q: Which feature of capillaries allows for efficient exchange of materials with surrounding tissues?
options: Thick muscular walls that regulate blood flow
Large lumen diameter to reduce flow resistance
Presence of valves to prevent backflow
Fenestrated endothelium and walls only one cell thick
explanation: Fenestrated (pore-containing) capillaries with a single-cell-thick wall allow rapid diffusion of gases, nutrients, and wastes between blood and tissue fluid.
